questions.text: A patient is treated with a β-lactam antibiotic, but the infection persists. Analysis shows the bacterium has a thin peptidoglycan layer and an outer membrane containing lipopolysaccharides. Which explains the resistance?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Gram-positive bacterium lacking an outer membrane
Gram-negative bacterium with outer membrane blocking the antibiotic
options.explanation: Gram-negative bacteria have an outer membrane that prevents β-lactam antibiotics from reaching the peptidoglycan layer. Gram-positive bacteria are generally more sensitive due to thick peptidoglycan exposure. The description of a 'thin peptidoglycan layer' and an 'outer membrane with lipopolysaccharides' is diagnostic of a Gram-negative bacterium.

options.text: Bacterium producing endospores that neutralize the antibiotic
Bacterium carrying plasmids coding for chlorophyll

questions.explanation: Gram-negative bacteria have an outer membrane that prevents β-lactam antibiotics from reaching the peptidoglycan layer. questions.text: A bacterium moves toward nutrient-rich zones and aligns itself along the Earth’s magnetic field. Which types of locomotion does it exhibit?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chemotaxis enables movement toward chemical gradients, while magnetotaxis allows orientation along magnetic field lines. Both can operate simultaneously in some bacteria.

questions.explanation: Chemotaxis enables movement toward chemical gradients, while magnetotaxis allows orientation along magnetic field lines. Both can operate simultaneously in some bacteria.

questions.text: A patient develops sepsis. The bacterial culture is Gram-negative, and antibiotics targeting peptidoglycan fail. Which structural feature most likely prevents antibiotic entry?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gram-negative bacteria have an outer membrane containing lipopolysaccharides that acts as a barrier, reducing access of antibiotics to peptidoglycan.

questions.explanation: Gram-negative bacteria have an outer membrane containing lipopolysaccharides that acts as a barrier, reducing access of antibiotics to peptidoglycan.
